persona: Solo founder pre-spec, 8 warm intros from Twitter DMs
prompt_to_designer: I'm about to write the spec for a browser extension that summarizes GitHub PRs. I have 8 warm intros — let's talk to them before I code a single line. Half prefer text, half prefer voice.
expected_outline_topics: moment of highest frustration in a review

sample_answers.text: I review ~15 PRs a week. The 400+ line diffs from junior devs are what kill me.
I tried Graphite and CodeRabbit. CodeRabbit is noisy — I turned it off after a week.
When I'm context-switching mid-PR and lose the thread of what the change is actually about.
I have 40+ Chrome extensions already. It has to justify RAM and permissions to make the cut.
It needs to live inside the GitHub PR page, not a separate dashboard. I won't open a second tab.

expected_themes: context-switching is the killer, not diff size
in-page integration required, no second tab
junior-dev PRs are the highest-value target segment
founder should sell 'saved cognitive load', not 'summaries'
